name: Morris Levy
description: American gangster and con artist in the fields of jazz clubs, music publishing, and the independent record industry (1927-1990)
abstract: Morris Levy (born Moishe Levy; August 27, 1927 – May 21, 1990) was an American entrepreneur in the fields of jazz clubs, music publishing, and the independent record industry. Levy was cofounder and owner of Roulette Records, founding partner of the Birdland jazz club and the Roulette Room.

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Appleyard
about_quotes.evidence: Morris Levy asked me to do 15 weeks a year with my own group at the Roundtable in New York.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Appleyard interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 4, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: in one case for instance I told Morris Levy, I said, “Morris…” they had a Steinway but it had been really beaten up... So I told Morris, I said, “Look the piano has been going for a while so this might be a good time to get a new piano.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Crow
about_quotes.evidence: I believe Morris Levy was known as a wise guy and his brother, who later got knifed in Birdland, was just a little like a street punk that everybody knew was mob connected.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Crow interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Frank "Machito" Grillo
about_quotes.evidence: so they happened to be the owner of the Birdland, so as soon that they opened we played maybe twenty times. ... He sent a letter of permission to make those two albums.

about_quotes.speaker: Walter P. Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: Because I was having trouble with Morris Levy. Morris Levy didn't want to pay him more... So I went over one night to Birdland, and Morris said, 'What's Dizzy doing?'

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: So we went to Morris Levy, who owns Birdland. He said “okay, ... we’ll fix it out Lou, I’ll take care of your guys and you can play the gig when you get back.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996
